principle: Relations are typed and directional. A connection means the linked evidence addresses the relation; it does not imply universal causality.

mechanisms.label: Labile iron & iron homeostasis
mechanisms.description: Iron uptake, storage, release, compartmentalization and chelatable Fe²⁺.

mechanisms.label: PUFA phospholipid remodelling
mechanisms.description: Formation, transport and depletion of oxidizable membrane phospholipid substrates.

mechanisms.label: Lipid radical-chain oxidation
mechanisms.description: Initiation, propagation and termination of phospholipid peroxidation.

mechanisms.label: GPX4–glutathione defence
mechanisms.description: Selenium-dependent reduction of phospholipid hydroperoxides and its metabolic support.

mechanisms.label: FSP1–CoQ defence
mechanisms.description: GPX4-independent membrane radical control through quinone redox systems.

mechanisms.label: Organelle and membrane geography
mechanisms.description: Where oxidation, iron release and defence occur and how signals move between compartments.

mechanisms.label: Membrane failure and death execution
mechanisms.description: The transition from lipid damage to ion imbalance, rupture and population propagation.

mechanisms.label: Tumour state and microenvironment
mechanisms.description: How genotype, nutrients, oxygen, immunity and anatomical niche reshape sensitivity.

mechanisms.label: Tissue injury and organ protection
mechanisms.description: Ferroptosis-related damage in kidney, brain, grafts and other physiological contexts.

mechanisms.label: Chemical intervention and translation
mechanisms.description: Inducers, inhibitors, target engagement, exposure and treatment windows.

mechanismEdges.label: PUFA-PL expands the oxidizable membrane pool
uncontrolled chain oxidation precedes membrane failure
lysosomes and other compartments shape reactive iron
ER, lysosome and plasma membrane conclusions depend on condition
cell state and niche remodel lipid substrates
FSP1 targetability varies by tumour and anatomical site
mechanismEdges.confidence: strong in selected in vivo models

mechanismEdges.label: requires tissue-level genetic and chemical evidence
GPX4 and cystine metabolism are common experimental entry points
FSP1 inhibition is advancing in defined tumour models
iron chelation, delivery and compartment activation have opposite goals
local perfusion and organ protection do not yet equal clinical efficacy
